summary: No P0/P1 blockers found. The helper extraction in pr_merge_readiness.py reads as behavior-preserving for the reviewed paths, and the missing raw-output hash regression asserts the precise blocker label rather than only the final blocked state. I found one nonblocking validation consistency gap around review_packet_path self-binding.

findings.title: Packet self-path is trusted when validating reviewer report context
evidence: scripts/reviewers/prepare_review_set_artifacts.py:377-379 writes the per-reviewer review_packet_path into each generated packet and treats it as an envelope field.
scripts/repo_validation/review.py:777-780 validates reviewer_instance_id inside each packet but does not validate packet.review_packet_path against the actual inputs.review_packets path.

scripts/repo_validation/review.py:209-215 and scripts/repo_validation/pr_merge_readiness.py:556-562 use packet.review_packet_path as the expected reviewer-report review_context value, falling back to the actual packet path only when the packet field is absent.
findings.why_it_matters: A stale or hand-authored packet can carry an incorrect review_packet_path, and a reviewer report that copies that value can still pass context validation. That leaves a misleading packet reference even though the contract assignment points elsewhere. This does not appear to break the current generated artifacts because the preparation helper writes the correct value.
findings.recommendation: When validating packet artifacts or reviewer-report context, require packet.review_packet_path to equal the resolved assignment/default packet path. Add a regression that mutates only packet.review_packet_path and the report context to the same wrong value.

self_declared_limitations: Read-only review only; I did not run tests, scripts, package managers, network commands or git mutations.
I reviewed the provided packet, rendered prompt and referenced repository artifacts, and treated excluded stale review artifacts as historical context only.
